mentions.note: Insider buying by the CEO, a board member, and Tim Cook signals a genuine business turnaround.
Cramer growing impatient but not selling; expects $70 target eventually; huge turnaround potential but patience required. Discussing further Friday.
Down 22% YTD; no line of sight to greatness yet; China intractable, competition fevered, old inventory still problematic; hurting trust.

Caller Blake. Cramer owns for travel trust; down 32% YoY, one of his bigger mistakes. Elliott Hill's turnaround happening but too slowly. No floor signs yet. Wait for catalyst/turnaround proof before buying more.

CNBC Investing Club wrong on Nike; Piper downgrade today cited lack of innovation under returning CEO Elliott Hill; company has more problems than realized; turn difficult; Cramer been wrong not early; gave until October (2 years in) but admits error in judgment.
Iconic brand in crisis: revenue down 3%, Direct down 7%, Converse down 35% (catastrophic), gross margin contracted 130bps to 40.2%. Elliott Hill turnaround moving slower than needed.
Disappointing performance; discussed at club meeting yesterday. Giving company one more quarter for redemption; if they disappoint again, have to sell despite long hold period and sunk capital.
5% rally on beaten-down quarter unjustified—no guidance raise, consumer pressure unabated; trust dumped stock; expect stall-out as no earnings bump forward; global competition mounting.
